Empires & Puzzles Cecilia Review: The Twilight Blade
Cecilia brings fast-mana dodges and boosted health to the table, but can her Clash of Knights stats keep up with the modern meta? We break down her viability.
Cecilia Review: Fast Swords & Faster Feet
Cecilia: The Twilight Blade Who Dodges Everything... Except Power Creep
Cecilia is a 5-star Dark (Purple) hero from the

Alliance Quest, released back in the ancient times of April 2024. As a member of the Stag Family, she was designed to be a sleek, fast-mana nuisance who combines AOE damage with the ever-annoying Dodge mechanic. She sits in a crowded archetype—the "Fast Purple Dodger"—rubbing shoulders with the likes of

While she doesn't have a costume to update her wardrobe or her stats, she remains a mechanically solid hero. If you enjoy watching enemy snipers miss their shots and heal your team instead, Cecilia is your kind of elf. Just don't ask her to arm wrestle the new Constructs; it won't end well.

Let's give credit where it's due: Cecilia has some of the best artwork in the Clash of Knights roster. She embodies the "High Fantasy Elf Warrior" aesthetic perfectly, with a dynamic pose, a massive sword that probably weighs more than she does, and an expression that says, "I'm about to sidestep your entire special attack." The color palette of regal purples and golds fits the Dark element beautifully without being the usual "skulls and gloom" vibe. It’s elegant, sharp, and polished—much like her playstyle.
Here is where we have to have a "family meeting" about power creep. Cecilia was released in early 2024, and by 2026 standards, her base stats are showing their age like milk left out in the sun.
Her base Attack (~1030) and Defense (~1050) are roughly
. Her health pool of 1771 is nearly

the current 2200+ standard. Even with a full Limit Break (LB2), her power (1239) barely scratches the surface of a non-limit-broken modern hero. She relies entirely on her Dodge to survive; if she gets hit, she breaks.
Cecilia's special is a classic "Support wrapped in Attacker clothing."
At Fast speed, this used to be respectable. Today, it's essentially a "softening" blow or a way to finish off critically wounded enemies. Don't expect her to wipe teams.
This is the main course. A 45% chance to dodge Special Skills for 3 turns is statistically significant. It forces the enemy to rely on tile damage or luck.
This is the cherry on top. Every time an ally dodges, they get 250 Boosted Health. Against multi-hitters (like chain attackers or AOE), this can generate a massive amount of HP swing.
variant. Bastet deals less damage (usually) but gives a Defense ailment to enemies. Cecilia focuses more on self-sustain with the healing.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
bonus is excellent for offensive stability. If an enemy tries to apply an Attack Down ailment (like from a Barbarian talent or a debuffer), it gets
. This makes her immune to being neutered by attack debuffs, ensuring her chip damage always lands.

Recovering 5% health when receiving a buff is a nice survival tool. Since she buffs herself (and the team) with Dodge, she guarantees a small self-heal every time she fires. Pair her with other buffers, and she becomes surprisingly durable despite her lower stats.

At the start of battle, she gets a 20% Attack boost. This helps patch up her outdated attack stat for the first 6 turns, making her initial slash sting a bit more. It synergizes well with her Fast speed, as she will likely fire while this buff is active.
Upon release, Cecilia was seen as a solid, if unoriginal, addition. She was immediately compared to
. Players loved the art but lamented the beta nerf (where the healing was 500 per dodge!).
The game has moved towards "Sure Hit" mechanics (Owls, Astral Elves) specifically to counter this Dodge meta. Additionally, raw stats have exploded.

The ability to give the entire team a 45% survival chance at Fast speed is always powerful.
The healing is "Boosted," meaning it works even if your team is full HP, acting as a pseudo-shield.
The Stag family bonus makes her immune to Attack Down, keeping her tile damage relevant.
The chance to bypass defensive buffs (like Riposte or Defense Up) is valuable on an AOE hit.

If you pulled Cecilia, congratulations! She is an excellent hero for a growing roster. Her dodge will carry you through difficult map stages and help you punch above your weight in Raids. Prioritize leveling her, as she is versatile enough to be on your Defense and Attack teams.

She is a luxury depth piece. Unless you really need a Dodge hero for War depth or Bloody Battle tournaments, she probably doesn't get the Limit Break 2 (Alpha Aether) materials. The stats are just too low to justify the expense compared to a new Construct or Astral hero.
The community had mixed feelings upon her release:
"Why did they nerf the boosted health from 500 to 250? 250 is nothing."
- The beta nerf was a major sore point.
"She’s my first dodge hero... It’s a huge addition for my roster."
- Proof that for non-whales, she was (and is) a game-changer.
"Reminds me of C. Kadilen with higher stats and Love the artwork on most CoK heroes"
- A common sentiment; she was the "modern" Kadilen... for about 3 months.

Cecilia is like a beautiful, vintage sports car. She looks stunning, handles corners (dodges) beautifully, but lacks the raw horsepower of the modern monster trucks dominating the highway. She won't single-handedly win you the game with brute force, but she might just frustrate your opponent enough to make them flee.
in the current era. Worth maxing if you lack a 5-star Dodger, but keep her away from the Owls.
